signals.headline: US Imposes Blockade on Iranian Ports as Ceasefire Talks Stall | ESCALATION CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US Imposes Blockade on Iranian Ports as Ceasefire Talks Stall | ESCALATION CONFIRMED | HIGH confidence**

President Trump has ordered the U.S. Central Command to maintain a blockade of all Iranian ports, with the Navy restricting maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.[3][4] This blockade specifically targets Iranian oil tankers while allowing other international transit under monitoring.[4

signals.headline: Two-Week Ceasefire Announced April 7 Remains in Effect but Expires Early Next Week | STILL ACTIVE | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Two-Week Ceasefire Announced April 7 Remains in Effect but Expires Early Next Week | STILL ACTIVE | HIGH confidence**

A two-week ceasefire was announced on April 7 and is set to expire in early the following week.[2] However, both the U.S. and Iran are discussing fresh negotiations before the ceasefire expires, with talks previously stalling over Iran's enriched uranium stockpile.[2][4]
